understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve the level within the step budget by moving a selected piece around the grid (likely to reach a goal tile/position) while possibly using multiple selectable pieces to manipulate the layout.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions shifted the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction. If a pushable object was directly adjacent in the movement direction, the move could push it along with the selected piece (provided space/constraints allowed). Walls and boundaries could block movement; this was evident when repeated MOVE_RIGHT attempts stopped changing position at [18, 21], indicating an obstacle or boundary to the right. Selection was done via CLICK on the pixel-coordinate position of another selectable, which instantly switched control to that piece (e.g., clicking [18, 21] or [36, 21] changed the active piece and subsequent moves applied to it).
